opportunities.title: Poland's Grid Went Dark, Brussels Named Names: Inside the First Joint EU-UK Russia Cyber Sanctions Package
opportunities.why_now: EU and UK officially attributed the Polish power grid attack to Russian intelligence the same day sanctions and GRU officer charges dropped, giving a rare attribution-to-consequence timeline to dissect.
opportunities.angle: Timeline explainer: attack → attribution → sanctions, with what 'critical infrastructure hardening' actually means for utilities

opportunities.title: Progress Just Killed ShareFile Servers Overnight — and Won't Say Why
opportunities.why_now: An emergency shutdown order with no disclosed CVE or exploit details is exactly the kind of 'mystery threat' that fuels the inevitable-breach narrative and demands admin action now.
opportunities.angle: Breaking-news brief: what's known, what's speculated, immediate mitigation steps for ShareFile Storage Zone Controller admins

opportunities.title: Your Mac's Own Crash Reporter Is Lying to You: CrashStealer Slips Past Gatekeeper With Apple's Blessing
opportunities.why_now: A notarized dropper defeating macOS's core trust mechanism is a rare and alarming platform-security failure worth flagging to Mac-using orgs immediately.
opportunities.angle: Technical breakdown of the notarization bypass + why 'Apple signed it' is no longer a safety signal

opportunities.title: CISA Says Joomla Extensions Are Being Popped as Zero-Days — Here's Your Patch List
opportunities.why_now: CISA's active-exploitation warning on iCagenda and Balbooa Forms flaws is a direct, actionable patch-cadence story with a hard deadline for site admins.
opportunities.angle: Patch-now digest: affected extensions, exploit status, and a checklist for Joomla site owners

opportunities.title: A Popular npm Security Package Just Got Backdoored to Install a Rust Infostealer (Yes, Ironic)
opportunities.why_now: Jscrambler — a code-obfuscation/security tool — shipping malware in its own npm release is a striking supply-chain irony worth spotlighting for dev-facing readers.
opportunities.angle: Supply-chain post-mortem: how the compromise happened, IOC list, and why 'security tooling' isn't immune
